patch.value: User request 2026-05-17: remove the need to manually type scope=project in commands/options where the PM path already identifies the project context. Project scope should be the default for project-local operations. Global scope should remain opt-in for global pm package operations only. This should reduce token use and CLI friction for agents while preserving backwards compatibility.
Default project scope for files/docs/tests and simplify scope UX

Make project scope the implicit default for non-package linked-resource commands so agents do not need to pass scope=project for files, docs, tests, comments, notes, or other project-local mutations. Keep explicit global/project scope only where it is meaningful for pm package operations, such as installing or managing global pm packages.

files/docs/test linked-resource commands accept entries without scope=project and persist project scope by default; help/contracts/examples stop requiring scope=project for project-local commands; package install/manage flows still allow explicit project/global scope where meaningful; backwards compatibility preserves existing scope=project inputs; focused tests and temp-project dogfood cover the simplified UX.

Creating this child under pm-rnpb.
